prompt_text: Sprinto vs Drata pricing and features
response:
Sprinto is generally the **lower-cost, startup-oriented** option, while Drata is usually **more expensive** but offers a more polished enterprise-style compliance experience.[1][2][6] Both platforms cover major frameworks like **SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and GDPR**, but Drata is typically positioned more toward mid-market and enterprise teams, while Sprinto is aimed at faster audit readiness for smaller or growing companies.[2][3][4][6]

| Category | **Sprinto** | **Drata** |
|---|---|---|
| **Pricing** | Commonly reported around **$5,000–$12,000/year** for simpler plans, with multi-framework bundles often **$15,000–$25,000+**.[1][2][3] | Commonly reported around **$10,000–$15,000/year** at the low end, with many quotes rising to **$20,000–$50,000+** depending on size and add-ons.[1][2][4] |
| **Pricing model** | Usually sold via sales quote; some sources describe **simpler or more transparent** pricing than Drata, with unlimited users in some tiers.[1][3] | **Custom quote** / contact sales; pricing is often described as less transparent and more dependent on team size and modules.[3][5] |
| **Framework coverage** | Supports major frameworks and, in some sources, **20+ frameworks** with reusable controls across frameworks.[2][3] | Supports major frameworks and is described as covering **SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, PCI DSS**, and more.[2][3][5] |
| **Automation** | Strong emphasis on **automated evidence collection**, guided workflows, and control reuse (“Magic Mapping”).[2][3] | Strong on **continuous compliance monitoring**, automated evidence collection, and real-time posture visibility.[2][3][4] |
| **Integrations** | Typically reported at **100+ to 200+ integrations**.[2][3][4] | Typically reported at **100+ to 140+ integrations**.[2][4][5] |
| **Best fit** | **Startups and smaller teams** that want faster certification and lower spend.[2][3][6] | **Mid-market and larger teams** that want stronger dashboards, reporting, and enterprise polish.[2][3][6] |

A practical way to choose: if your top priority is **budget and speed to audit readiness**, Sprinto is usually the better fit.[1][2][3] If your top priority is **deeper dashboarding, enterprise-style workflow, and a more mature compliance operating model**, Drata is usually the stronger option.[2][3][4][6]

A few caveats matter here: both vendors use **custom pricing**, so actual quotes can vary widely based on company size, number of frameworks, support level, and add-ons.[1][3][4] Also, third-party comparison sites disagree somewhat on exact integration counts and pricing bands, so the ranges above should be treated as **market estimates**, not official list prices.[2][3][4][5]

If you want, I can also give you a **feature-by-feature side-by-side** for specific needs like **SOC 2**, **ISO 27001**, **HIPAA**, or **startup pricing negotiations**.
